svg.text: JavaScript (live-panel.js, socket.js, app.js, kanban.js) — all synchronous, single-threaded
Can be restarted without killing sessions. All session calls proxy to daemon.
async_mode='threading' — each WS event gets a pool thread
Reads JSON lines from daemon TCP socket
Blocking socket.recv() loop — never touches SocketIO directly
Decoupled so slow WebSocket writes never block IPC reads
Sends JSON request, blocks on threading.Event
until daemon-reader thread delivers matching response
PROCESS 2 — Session Daemon — port 5051 (survives web restarts)
Detached subprocess. Owns all Claude sessions. Crash recovery from ~/.claude/gui_active_sessions.json
Reads requests from Flask TCP socket
Spawns a worker thread per request so nothing blocks
Set state synchronously, then dispatch async work ▼
ASYNC EVENT LOOP — single dedicated thread (session-manager-loop)
All sessions share this one loop. Coroutines yield at await points. No threading bugs between sessions.
File snapshots · JSONL parsing · directory rglob
Called via await loop.run_in_executor() — doesn't block event loop
Keep stdin open · permission format · parse tolerance
Each session owns its own CLI subprocess via SDK connect()
CLI SUBPROCESSES — one claude process per session
Spawned by ClaudeSDKClient.connect(). Communicate with daemon via stdin/stdout pipes.
anyio buffer: 100 msgs, shared across turns
anyio buffer: 100 msgs, shared across turns
anyio buffer: 100 msgs, shared across turns
Each CLI subprocess maintains its own authenticated connection to Claude
DATA FLOW: user sends "Fix the bug"
Flask SocketIO handler receives on pool thread
DaemonClient sends JSON request over TCP, blocks
Messages stream back: _emit_entry() → TCP → queue → emit
Browser receives session_entry events, updates UI live
ResultMessage arrives → state=IDLE → dispatches queued msgs
